**My Dearest Eliza,**

I write these words with a trembling hand and a heart heavy with the weight of years unspoken. Five long years, and the storm is upon us now, and I fear for my life, but more than that, I fear for you. You were once my light in the dark, but now...now I am alone, and the world feels colder than the wind that howls through the cracks in this old lighthouse.

I have not seen you since the day you left, and I have not spoken since that day. I have tried to forget, but the memories of you are like ghosts that won't leave me. I remember your laugh, your voice, the way you would dance the waves, and the way you would light up the fire when it was cold. I remember the way you would smile even when the world seemed to hate you.

But now...now I am alone, and I have to face this storm. I have to keep the light burning, and I have to keep the light burning for you, for your eyes to see,for your heart to feel. I have to be strong, even if the sea takes everything, even if the wind takes my breath.

I know I can't save you, but I can't let go. I can't let the memories of you go. I can't let the pain of your absence go. I can't let the silence go. I can't let the cold go.
